metric_value: The TPC is a place where business IT seekers can go with greater confidence for numbers without fear of stacked configuration decks and misleading numbers.
notes: Kastner quote; identified as analyst for Vericours

metric_value: Every vendor had specific tests, but buyers were confused and sometimes misled. There was lots of truth in the maxim 'Lies, damned lies, and benchmarks.'
As a result of TPC-induced competition, the industry has seen application-level performance improvements of 100-fold.

The secret to the success of TPC benchmarks is that the specifications are set by a committee of benchmark-jaded engineers from Dell, HP, IBM, Sun, Oracle, and Microsoft. These men and women fight competitively every day — and lots of nights.
The combination of detailed benchmark standards, an independent audit, and in-the-sunlight peer review makes the TPC benchmarks the gold standard of commercial benchmarks.
TPC announced an OLTP benchmark — TPC-E — with expanded workload modeled on brokerage houses, reflecting more contemporary workloads than TPC-C.
TPC announced a proposed Web-service benchmark with database interactions and durable message-queue operations displaying ACID properties against a commercial application server.
TPC announced a proposed decision-support benchmark measuring a randomized set of transactions.
notes: Roadmap from TPC; later realized as TPC-DS (2012)

metric_value: Kastner cited as 'analyst for Vericours' — not Aberdeen Group. Likely a post-Aberdeen consulting affiliation circa 2005-2007; may also reflect editorial error.
TPC-E was formally released in February 2007 — fulfilling the 2005 roadmap Kastner previewed.
